pullRequest.title: DOM-54210 bitbucket datacenter PAT access DOM-55142 rename to data center

nodes.body:
test values for http://bb-datacenter.workbench-analyze-team-sandbox.domino.tech:7990/users/roshikiri/repos/tester-1/browse

credentialsa re in onepassword

this validation is happening on the repoman side as well
https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/pull/39614/files#diff-ab35f39f969b8c07ffd1924071cdf08fcd5e6097b7420106838fe8bb62daf422R86
however, the retry logic is not
in theory we should remove sslVerify=false but we can't do that for testing on our domino bitbucket enterprise until we pay for a legit not-self-signed SSL cert
Some would say that you should just use an if statement here
this is run in `/tmp` because we don't want to run commands from the root directory and we know that `/tmp` exists
For all of the prinlns, can we use a logger with error log level? The println will log this message all of the time and that will make the logs noisy.
Since exceptions are being thrown, the printlns were primarily used for debugging so I'll just remove them
Can we add a unit test that verifies that this "noVerify" is only added for bitbucket datacenter?
Is it possible for bitbucket datacenter uri to not contain port?
this ssl no verify tag is used solely for insecure https connections like for our testing env. There might be a better way to filter these out from customer's production setups without a port

maybe we should just put a cert on the test deployment? Having a case where we don't verify ssl means there is a case where we could send data to an unknown party and also in plain text.  @SeanW-DDL do we usually add TLS to our test deployments?
can we comment with a ticket ID for removing this when it's possible to do so?
since jobs is using the injected module in `server/` this needs to stay in place
